text: Listening to these reflections in the mostly empty seminar space, I am struck by a bittersweet sense of arriving somewhere meaningful after a long, winding road. The journey feels both fulfilling and slightly mournful—so much time passed, and yet there is a hunger for more, for deeper experience and understanding. Moving to the exhibition, I sense an echo of this feeling: curiosity paired with mild regret for what I have not yet seen or grasped. I am both content and restless, suspended between gratitude for what is and yearning for what might still unfold.
